rules.title: Research-First Document Creation
rules.description: When creating or updating complex documents for the vectorstore (tool guides, RAG scripts, skill guides, system prompts), NEVER rely solely on internal/base knowledge. These documents contain version-specific Blender Python API details, breaking changes, and domain-specific best practices that require verified, up-to-date sources.
steps: 1. ALWAYS research first using NotebookLM (preferred) or Context7/Firecrawl before writing any tool guide, RAG script, or vectorstore document.
2. Create a dedicated NotebookLM notebook for the research topic.
3. Run fast or deep research to discover relevant sources.
4. Import sources and query the notebook for specific, citation-backed answers.
5. Write the document based ONLY on research findings, not base knowledge.
6. Process ONE document at a time: research → query → write → commit → next document.
7. Do NOT batch-fill context with all research at once — query per document for best results.
8. Always verify Blender Python API calls against the target version (currently 5.x).
9. Flag any content that came from base knowledge vs research sources.
